header.title: Robots.txt Generator — Control Search Engine Crawling Easily
header.description: Free online robots.txt generator to create robots.txt files that control how search engines crawl and index your website. Add allow and disallow rules for specific paths, set crawl delay, specify sitemap location, and add rules for individual user-agents like Googlebot and Bingbot.
header.keywords: robots.txt generator, robots txt file, seo crawl control, robots txt creator, googlebot rules, block search engine crawling

rules.title: Crawl Rules
rules.noBlocked: No blocked paths — add one above

sitemaps.title: Sitemaps
sitemaps.noSitemaps: No sitemaps added — recommended to include your sitemap URL

validation.title: Validation
validation.orphanDirective: Disallow/Allow directive found without a preceding User-agent
validation.invalidDelay: Crawl-delay must be a positive number
validation.highDelay: Crawl-delay of {val}s is very high — may hurt indexing
validation.emptyDisallow: Empty Disallow means allow all crawling (this is correct)
validation.blockAllWarning: Disallow: / blocks all crawling — make sure this is intentional

howToUse.title: How to Use Robots.txt Generator
tips: Always include a User-agent directive before adding rules
Use * for rules that apply to all crawlers
Test your robots.txt in Google Search Console
Remember to upload the file to your root directory
howToUse.step1Desc: Specify the search engine crawler (e.g., Googlebot, Bingbot, or * for all)
howToUse.step2Desc: Add Disallow and Allow rules to control which paths each crawler can access
howToUse.step3Desc: Include your sitemap URLs so crawlers can discover all your pages
howToUse.step4Desc: Download the robots.txt file and upload it to your website root directory

deployment.tip: Upload robots.txt to your website root at {url}. Changes take effect on next crawl.

allowAll.label: Allow all
allowAll.desc: Allow all robots to crawl everything

blockAll.label: Block all
blockAll.desc: Block all robots from all pages

examples.title: Try an Example
examples.sampleText:
User-agent: *
Allow: /

User-agent: GPTBot
Disallow: /

Sitemap: https://example.com/sitemap.xml
examples.result: ✓ Valid robots.txt — allows all crawlers except GPTBot

items.question: What is robots.txt?
items.answer: Robots.txt is a text file placed in a website's root directory that instructs search engine crawlers which pages or sections to crawl and which to ignore.

items.question: Can robots.txt block indexing?
items.answer: No, robots.txt only prevents crawling, not indexing. If external sites link to a blocked page, it may still appear in search results. Use noindex tags to prevent indexing.

items.question: Where should robots.txt be placed?
items.answer: Robots.txt must be placed in the root directory of your website, typically at example.com/robots.txt. It cannot be placed in subdirectories.

items.question: Can all crawlers be blocked?
items.answer: While you can set Disallow for all user-agents, malicious crawlers may ignore robots.txt. It is a voluntary protocol and not a security measure.
